question: What are the admissions deadlines for SUTD?
answer: The application period for local Diploma applicants for SUTD is from January 2 to March 2, 2026.

question: Is there financial aid available?
answer: Yes, there is financial aid available at SUTD. Students can apply for scholarships administered by SUTD, including donor-sponsored scholarships, as well as financial aid. For more information, you can contact the Office of Student Administration at SUTD.

Overview
SUTD adopts a cohort-based fixed fee model whereby the tuition fee for a cohort intake will remain unchanged throughout the normal candidature of students. However, tuition fees for every new cohort are subject to change from year to year.
With the MOE Tuition Grant provided by the Government of Singapore, eligible students will get to enjoy subsidised tuition fees.
Besides tuition fees, other educational expenses include compulsory miscellaneous fees and housing fees. The annual compulsory miscellaneous fees cover registration to student council, students’ activities, insurance, sports facilities, IT facilities and academic related services.

question: What is the minimum score for the Mother Tongue Language?
answer: The minimum score for the Mother Tongue Language (MTL) requirement for admission into full-time publicly-funded undergraduate programmes at SUTD is a D7 grade for Higher MTL at Singapore-Cambridge GCE O-Level. Alternatively, a pass in MTL ‘B’ or a S grade for H1 MTL/MTL-in-lieu or H2 MTL Language and Literature or H1 General Studies in Chinese at Singapore-Cambridge GCE A-Level also fulfills the requirement.

Overview
International qualifications
Criteria for Admission
You should have completed at least 12 years of general education or have taken the Year 12 national/international examination in the year of application in order to be considered for admission. Students with strong competency in Mathematics and the Sciences, i.e. Physics or Chemistry, are generally preferred.
Mother Tongue Language (MTL)
All Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents are required to fulfil the MTL requirement for admission into full-time publicly-funded undergraduate programmes in the universities.
The MTL requirement may be fulfilled through the following:
a D7 grade for Higher MTL at Singapore-Cambridge GCE O-Level (the iGCSE MTL First Language examination does not fulfil the requirement); or
a pass in MTL ‘B’ or a S grade for H1 MTL/ MTL-in-lieu or H2 MTL Language and Literature or H1 General Studies in Chinese at Singapore-Cambridge GCE A-Level; or

question: Do I require reference letters?
answer: Yes, you are required to provide reference letters as part of your application. You should list up to two referees who know you well academically or professionally, and you need to seek their consent before listing them. Additionally, you are required to upload any relevant testimonials or recommendation letters.

This section is optional except for certain International Qualifications.
Key in any additional test results, e.g. English proficiency test, if any or as required.
UPLOAD
a copy of the official test result, if any or as required.
Personal insight questions
Please provide your responses to the personal insight questions to present new and additional information that will be helpful for your application.
Recommendation/ Testimonial
List up to two referees and their contact details. Your referee(s) should be persons who know you well academically (e.g. your teacher from High School/Polytechnic) or professionally (your internship or work supervisor) and for at least half a year recently. Do seek your referee(s)’ consent before listing them.

top_source_2_text:
a pass in MTL A: Literature, or MTL A: Language and Literature, or Language B MTL at Standard or Higher Level at International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (the IB Standard Level Language ab initio does not fulfil the requirement).
Those who have not fulfilled the MTL requirement may still apply for admission with no prejudice to their application. However, if accepted, they will be required to fulfil the MTL requirement during their course of study.
Applicants who have been away from Singapore’s school system for some years and have not kept up with the study of their MTL or a language that can be offered as MTL-in-lieu may apply for MTL exemption. The MTL exemption application will be facilitated by the universities as part of the university application, and the results made known to applicants who receive admission offers from the university. Please note that MOE does not accept direct applications for MTL exemption. The application for MTL exemption will also not prejudice the evaluation of the application for a place in the university.

question: Can polytechnic diploma students apply?
answer: Yes, polytechnic diploma students can apply to SUTD. Regardless of nationality, applicants with diplomas from specific polytechnics or institutions in Singapore are eligible to apply, provided they meet the admission requirements.

question: Do I need SAT score?
answer: The SAT score is optional for admission to SUTD. However, if you choose to submit it, you should indicate the SUTD Institution Code (6532) on your SAT registration forms so that the scores will be sent directly to the university.

question: How much are the tuition fees for Singaporeans?
answer:
The tuition fees for Singapore Citizens (SC) at SUTD are as follows:

- For Academic Year 2023: S$13,500 per academic year (S$6,750 per term)
- For Academic Year 2022: S$13,500 per academic year (S$6,750 per term)
- For Academic Year 2021: S$13,300 per academic year (S$6,650 per term)
- For Academic Year 2020: S$13,200 per academic year (S$6,600 per term)

These fees are inclusive of Goods and Services Tax (GST).

question: How much are the tuition fees for international students?
answer:
The tuition fees for international students at SUTD are as follows:

- **Academic Year 2020**: S$28,982 per academic year (S$14,491 per term)
- **Academic Year 2021**: S$29,236 per academic year (S$14,618 per term)
- **Academic Year 2022**: S$29,694 per academic year (S$14,847 per term)
- **Academic Year 2023**: S$30,176 per academic year (S$15,088 per term)
- **Academic Year 2024**: S$30,650 per academic year (S$15,325 per term)
- **Academic Year 2025**: S$31,400 per academic year (S$15,700 per term)
- **Academic Year 2026**: S$31,600 per academic year (S$15,800 per term)

These fees are inclusive of GST.

question: Is there a minimum CAP?
answer: The information regarding a minimum CAP (Cumulative Average Point) is not available in the provided documents.
top_source_1_text:
Electives (96 Credits)
Electives, provide the opportunity for students to customise the ISTD degree according to their interests and future careers.
AY2019 ISTD students are required to take a minimum of 4 ISTD electives in order to graduate from the ISTD degree.
AY2020 ISTD students (and subsequent batches) are required to take a minimum of 5 ISTD electives in order to graduate from the ISTD degree.
HASS (60 Credits)
Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (HASS) are essential component of an SUTD degree.
Students are required to take a HASS elective every term from Term 4 onwards.
Capstone (24 Credits)
The capstone is a culminating project that allows students to use the skills they have learned in ISTD in a real world industry or research project. The capstone projects often focus on interdisciplinary applications, solved by a team of students chosen appropriately from different pillars.
Extra Courses
top_source_2_text:
By the numbers
ESD Core (84 Credits)
The ESD core is composed of courses focused on various methods for analysis and modelling of engineering systems. These courses, shown in red, are offered every year in terms 4, 5, and 6. Full term courses are 12 credits and half term courses are 6 credits, corresponding to the number of weeks of instruction. Click on the courses in the diagram below for more information about each course.
Capstone (24 Credits)
The capstone is a culminating project that allows students to use the skills they have learned in ESD in a real world industry or research project. The capstone projects often focus on interdisciplinary applications, solved by a team of students chosen appropriately from different pillars.
Electives (72 Credits)
top_source_3_text:
HASS (60 Credits)
Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences (HASS) are an essential component of an SUTD degree. HASS courses, shown in purple, are required in all but one term of study. There are a variety of HASS courses that are a particular relevance to ESD. It is strongly recommended that ESD students supplement their technical engineering programme with HASS electives that build competence in economic reasoning. One recommended course is Microeconomics, the study of resource allocation under distributed economic decision making.
Extra Courses
From AY2017 onwards, students who have, and maintain, a GPA of 3.5 or above (and have passed all Freshmore subjects) are eligible to enroll for an additional course in terms 5, 6 and/or 7. Subject to the prerequisites and timing of the course, students may use this flexibility to take an additional HASS or elective course.

question: What do I actually need to submit for my portfolio?
answer:
For your portfolio submission to SUTD, you need to include the following:

1. **Outstanding/Relevant Achievements**: List your past experiences in leadership, research, internships, entrepreneurship programs, community service, and significant achievements from the last four years.

2. **Supporting Documents**: Upload any supporting documents related to the achievements listed.

3. **Personal Insight Questions**: Provide responses to personal insight questions to present additional information about yourself.

4. **Recommendation/Testimonial**: List up to two referees with their contact details, ensuring they are individuals who know you well academically or professionally.

5. **Co-Curricular Activities**: Include participation details in co-curricular activities and any notable accomplishments.

6. **Academic Transcripts and Certificates**: Upload your academic transcripts and certificates from secondary/junior high and senior high school levels.

7. **Supplementary Results**: Optionally, include any additional test results, such as English proficiency tests.

Make sure to follow the guidelines for each section and upload the required documents as specified.

question: Is it possible to get into SUTD with a polytechnic diploma?
answer: Yes, it is possible to get into SUTD with a polytechnic diploma. Diplomas from the School of Engineering, Information Technology, Architecture, or Sciences are generally considered more relevant and are assessed more favorably for admission. However, other diplomas may still be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Your grades in the advanced modules will also be taken into consideration. You should also possess a good pass in your Additional Mathematics and Science subjects, i.e. Physics or Chemistry, in the GCE O-Level or equivalent qualification.

Generally, diplomas from the School of Engineering, Information Technology, Architecture or Sciences are considered more relevant to SUTD’s courses and hence will be assessed more favourably for admission. However, other diplomas may still be considered on a case-by-case basis.

question: How are roommates matched in the hostel?
answer: Roommates in the hostel are matched based on the class or cluster they are assigned to, as well as their lifestyle habits. Freshmore students complete a lifestyle questionnaire during the housing acceptance process, which helps in pairing them with roommates who have similar lifestyle habits as far as possible.
top_source_1_text:
Room type: Double Room
Freshmore students are paired in double rooms according to the class/cluster they are assigned to. Students complete a lifestyle questionnaire as part of the housing acceptance process, and will be matched with roommates of similar lifestyle habits as far as possible.
Hostel facilities
Communal shower and bathroom facilities on each floor
Mini pantry with microwave, hot/cold water dispenser and sink on each floor
Communal kitchen (level 1) equipped with induction cookers, multifunction oven and vending machines
Pantry (level 9) with a 2‐burner induction cooker, microwave, hot water dispenser and vending machines
Laundry room (level 1) with washing machines and dryers (pay-per-use basis)
Laundry drying area (level 12)
Hostel lounge (level 1, block 57)
Viewing deck (level 12, blocks 55 and 59)
Recreational rooms
Study and discussion rooms
LAN point and WIFI
top_source_2_text:
Residents’ safety and welfare
SUTD reviews its security measures periodically to ensure the safety and well-being of our hostel residents, and has strengthened its security measures at the hostel over the years. This includes a 24/7 on-site security team, regular security patrols, monitoring of CCTVs (closed circuit television) and controlled access to blocks, common facilities and bathrooms. Residents are also regularly reminded on issues regarding their personal safety and security.
In addition, live-in staff and hostel student leaders play an important role in the education, outreach and engagement of student residents. Together, they help to foster a culture of care and respect within the hostel community. By bringing residents’ concerns to various teams at the University, their presence helps the University to have a better appreciation of the issues of importance to residents. Resident students are also able to reach out to them easily if they are in need of assistance.
Room type: Double Room
top_source_3_text:
From Academic Year 2026, Freshmore undergraduates are required to reside at student housing on campus during Terms 1 to 3. Designed as an integrated part of the Freshmore experience, residential living complements cohort-based learning in and out of classrooms and aims to foster a sense of community and ownership, building strong friendships that carry through the undergraduate years and beyond.
Freshmore students who would like to stay on after Term 3 may apply for housing together with other undergraduates during scheduled housing application windows, typically in February each year. Information on the housing application window will be published on the
Undergraduate housing page
by early February. Housing allocation will be guided by the Undergraduate Housing Allocation Scheme. More information on the scheme will be shared with incoming Freshmores after the start of the September term.

acceptance period to be advised

question: What does the school like out for in applicants?
answer:
SUTD looks for several key qualities and traits in applicants during their comprehensive application review process. These include:

1. **Academic Competency**: Strong performance in Mathematics and Sciences, particularly in relevant subjects like Engineering and Computing Mathematics.

2. **Qualities and Traits**:
   - **Growth Mindset**: Embracing challenges and learning from feedback.
   - **Collaboration**: Working effectively with others and valuing diverse perspectives.
   - **Curiosity/Open-mindedness**: Showing inquisitiveness and being receptive to new ideas.
   - **Drive/Self-motivation**: Being proactive and self-directed in learning.
   - **Resilience**: Persevering through setbacks and maintaining focus under pressure.
   - **Risk-taking**: Experimenting with new approaches and accepting failure as part of learning.

3. **Portfolios**: Participation in co-curricular activities, notable accomplishments, leadership positions, and strong teacher recommendations.

4. **Personal Insight Questions**: Responses to these questions help assess fit with SUTD's values.

5. **Interview**: For selected candidates, an interview with faculty or leaders provides additional insights.

Overall, SUTD seeks well-rounded individuals who demonstrate both academic strength and personal qualities that align with the university's values.

question: How much does it cost to study there per year?
answer:
The cost to study at SUTD per academic year varies based on the student's residency status. For the academic year 2025, the tuition fees are as follows:

- **Singapore Citizens (SC)**: S$13,500
- **Singapore Permanent Residents (SPR)**: S$19,350
- **ASEAN International Students**: S$30,200
- **All other International Students**: S$31,600

Additionally, students should consider other expenses such as compulsory miscellaneous fees, accommodation, meals, and personal expenses, which can significantly increase the total cost.

question: If I don't have a good MTL grade, can I still get in?
answer: Yes, you can still apply for admission even if you don't have a good MTL grade. However, if accepted, you will be required to fulfill the MTL requirement during your course of study.
top_source_1_text:
a pass in MTL A: Literature, or MTL A: Language and Literature, or Language B MTL at Standard or Higher Level at International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (the IB Standard Level Language ab initio does not fulfil the requirement).
Those who have not fulfilled the MTL requirement may still apply for admission with no prejudice to their application. However, if accepted, they will be required to fulfil the MTL requirement during their course of study.
Applicants who have been away from Singapore’s school system for some years and have not kept up with the study of their MTL or a language that can be offered as MTL-in-lieu may apply for MTL exemption. The MTL exemption application will be facilitated by the universities as part of the university application, and the results made known to applicants who receive admission offers from the university. Please note that MOE does not accept direct applications for MTL exemption. The application for MTL exemption will also not prejudice the evaluation of the application for a place in the university.

question: Does SUTD admissions rely solely on academic results?
answer: No, SUTD admissions do not rely solely on academic results. The admissions process is holistic, assessing students based on academic performance, qualities and traits that match SUTD values, and portfolios that may include co-curricular activities.

question: What exactly is the Computer Science course in SUTD about?
answer: The Computer Science course at SUTD is offered under the Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science and Design (CSD). This programme focuses on a computing-intensive curriculum that equips students with essential computational and mathematical tools for problem-solving using computers. It aims to instill algorithmic thinking and includes mandatory core courses, which are offered every year in Term 4 and 5. Additionally, students can choose from a variety of electives to tailor their studies. The curriculum emphasizes the integration of technology with design, preparing students to address complex challenges through hands-on learning and interdisciplinary approaches.

top_source_2_text: In tandem with this, Freshmore courses will also have a greater emphasis on helping students understand the interdisciplinary nature of the world we live in. As a result, SUTD students won’t just be equipped with AI skills, they will be trained to thoughtfully leverage on AI to create effective, practical and translatable solutions that are human-centred, as opposed to being tech-centred. They will generate high-fidelity Computer-Aided Design (CAD) files, produce functional code, or conduct rapid cost and strategic analyses as part of this basic training. This SUTD project-based approach also trains students to discern when to use AI as a tool, collaborate with it as a teammate, or choose not to use it at all.

question: What if my English is not good?
answer: If your English is not strong, you may still apply for admission to SUTD. However, you will be required to submit scores from an English proficiency test, such as IELTS, TOEFL, SAT, PTE Academic, ACT, or C1 Advanced, especially if your qualifications were not taught in English. Additionally, if you are accepted, you may need to fulfill the English proficiency requirement during your course of study. It’s important to prepare for and improve your English skills to succeed in the program.
